channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B

item.title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao Liang

Recently, a variational wave function based on the tensor representation of backflow corrections along with a Lanczos step has achieved state-of-the-art energy precision for Fermi-Hubbard-type models. However, solving the Fermi-Hubbard model remains challenging, and the validity of any method must b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155121] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Investigating the Fermi-Hubbard model by the tensor-backflow method
item.dc:creator: Xiao Liang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Dynamical correlations and nonequilibrium sum rules in photodoped Hubbard ladders
item.description:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Merhej, J. P. Hague, R. M. Konik, and A. J. A. James

Using matrix product state techniques we study the nonequilibrium dynamical response of the half-filled Hubbard ladder when subject to an optical pump. Optical pumping offers a way of producing and manipulating new strongly correlated phenomena by suppressing existing magnetic correlations. The ladd…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165119] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Exotic transition and reentrance on the 1/9 magnetization plateau in a spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Da-zhi Fang, Shi-Ju Ran, and Gang Su

With the intriguing coexistence of local magnetic orders and nonlocal valence bond patterns, unconventional magnetic plateau phases in geometrically frustrated quantum systems have attracted significant attention. This work investigates the spin-1/2 anisotropic kagome antiferromagnet, uncovering an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140405] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Sliding barrier effects on magnetoresistance and spin-transfer torque in $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$-based magnetic tunnel junctions
item.description:
Author(s): Baochun Wu, Jie Yang, Shiqi Liu, and Jing Lu

Electronic properties of van der Waals materials are highly sensitive to their stacking modes. To explore sliding effects in the two-dimensional (2D) spintronics, we construct a magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) using sliding bilayer graphene or $h$-BN as barriers and the recently synthesized room-temp…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134415] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Baochun Wu, Jie Yang, Shiqi Liu, and Jing Lu

Electronic properties of van der Waals materials are highly sensitive to their stacking modes. To explore sliding effects in the two-dimensional (2D) spintronics, we construct a magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) using sliding bilayer graphene or h-BN as barriers and the recently synthesized room-temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134415] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Sliding barrier effects on magnetoresistance and spin-transfer torque in $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$-based magnetic tunnel junctions

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Resonant dynamics of one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard model with time-dependent tensor electric fields
item.description:
Author(s): Jiali Zhang and Shaoliang Zhang

Recently, tensor gauge fields and their coupling to fracton phases of matter have attracted more and more research interest, and a series of novel quantum phenomena arising from the coupling has been predicted. Here, we propose a tunable platform using a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144305] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiali Zhang and Shaoliang Zhang

Recently, tensor gauge fields and their coupling to fracton phases of matter have attracted more and more research interest, and a series of novel quantum phenomena arising from the coupling has been predicted. Here, we propose a tunable platform using a one-dimensional dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144305] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026

item.title: Mechanism of the Mott-like insulator-to-metal transition of heavy-ion irradiated $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge)
item.description:
Author(s): Siyue Zhang, Yasuhiro Niwa, Haruto Ejiri, Jun Fujioka, Kazuya Harii, Satoru Okayasu, and Yuki Shiomi

It was recently reported that heavy-ion irradiation to layered ferromagnetic insulators $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge) produces amorphous ferromagnetic metals with approximately three times higher transition temperatures (${T}_{C}$) than those of single crystals. This high-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155116] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Siyue Zhang, Yasuhiro Niwa, Haruto Ejiri, Jun Fujioka, Kazuya Harii, Satoru Okayasu, and Yuki Shiomi

It was recently reported that heavy-ion irradiation to layered ferromagnetic insulators CrXTe3 (X=Si and Ge) produces amorphous ferromagnetic metals with approximately three times higher transition temperatures (TC) than those of single crystals. This high-TC metallic state is reminiscent of the cor…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155116] Published Thu Apr 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Mechanism of the Mott-like insulator-to-metal transition of heavy-ion irradiated $\mathrm{Cr}X{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ ($X=\mathrm{Si}$ and Ge)

item.title: Specific heat and density anomaly in the Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Habitzreuter, Willdauany C. de Freitas Silva, Eduardo O. Rizzatti, Thereza Paiva, and Marcia C. Barbosa

Understanding thermal properties of materials is fundamental to technological applications and to discovering new phenomena. In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

Understanding thermal properties of materials is fundamental to technological applications and to discovering new phenomena. In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Thermoelectric evidence of the electronic structure changes from the charge density wave transition in FeGe
item.description:
Author(s): Kaila Jenkins, Yuan Zhu, Dechen Zhang, Guoxin Zheng, Kuan-Wen Chen, Aaron Chan, Sijie Xu, Mason L. Klemm, Bin Gao, Ming Yi, Pengcheng Dai, and Lu Li

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165112] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kaila Jenkins, Yuan Zhu, Dechen Zhang, Guoxin Zheng, Kuan-Wen Chen, Aaron Chan, Sijie Xu, Mason L. Klemm, Bin Gao, Ming Yi, Pengcheng Dai, and Lu Li

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165112] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.description:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.dc:creator: Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.description:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.dc:creator: Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Holon metal, charge density wave metal, and chiral superconductor from doping a fractional Chern insulator and an ${\mathrm{SU}(3)}_{1}$ chiral spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the $ν=−2/3$ fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated meta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the ν=−2/3 fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted MoTe2. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated metal with low carrier…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Dynamical correlations and Mott localization in a paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ bulk crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory ($\mathrm{DFT}+\mathrm{DMFT}$) calculations, we explicitl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic VI3 crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory (DFT+DMFT) calculations, we explicitly demonstrate the importance of loca…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamical correlations and Mott localization in a paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ bulk crystal

item.title: Electron energy loss function in a graphene-hBN-graphene nanostructure
item.description:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of ${\mathrm{layer}}_{1}$-hBN-${\mathrm{layer}}_{2}$ nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of layer1-hBN-layer2 nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac fermion approaches and by exami…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Electron energy loss function in a graphene-hBN-graphene nanostructure
item.dc:creator: Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

item.title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.dc:creator: Yawar Mohammadi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
